heading: Recruitment incentives for placement at remote locations
label: 1599i Recruitment incentives for placement at remote locations
notes: Outcome Measurements, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–283, div. A, title XI, § 1120(b)134 Stat. 3899Jan. 1, 2021“The Secretary of Defense shall develop outcome measurements to evaluate the effect of the authority provided under subsection (a) of , United States Code, as added by subsection (a), and any relocation incentives provided under subsection (b) of such section.”section 1599i of title 10
amendments: Outcome Measurements, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–283, div. A, title XI, § 1120(b)134 Stat. 3899Jan. 1, 2021“The Secretary of Defense shall develop outcome measurements to evaluate the effect of the authority provided under subsection (a) of , United States Code, as added by subsection (a), and any relocation incentives provided under subsection (b) of such section.”section 1599i of title 10

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: An individual appointed to a position in the Department of Defense at a covered location may be paid a recruitment incentive in connection with such appointment.

children.label: A
children.text: 25 percent of the annual rate of basic pay of the employee for the position concerned as of the date on which the service period in such position agreed to by the individual under paragraph (3) commences; multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the number of years (including fractions of a year) of such service period (not to exceed four years).

children.heading: .—Service agreement
children.label: 3 .—Service agreement
children.text: To receive a recruitment incentive under this subsection, an individual appointed to a position under paragraph (1) shall enter into an agreement with the Secretary of Defense to complete a period of service at the covered location. The period of obligated service of the individual at such location under the agreement may not exceed four years. The agreement shall include such repayment or alternative employment obligations as the Secretary considers appropriate for failure of the individual to complete the period of obligated service specified in the agreement.

children.heading: .—Covered locations defined
children.label: 4 .—Covered locations defined
children.text: In this section, a covered location is a location for which the Secretary of Defense has determined that critical hiring needs are not being met due to the geographic remoteness or isolation or extreme climate conditions of the location.

children.heading: .—Sunset
children.label: b .—Sunset
children.text: Effective on , the authority provided under subsection (a) shall expire.September 30, 2022
